new client inquiry, ways to work together, consult, follow-up

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • Keep multiple offer paths from becoming multiple inbox threads.
  • See who still needs a reply before the inquiry goes cold.
  • Track consult status and due-date follow-up in the same workspace.

1. Today Ops Snapshot

Today Ops Snapshot

Shows the daily working hub: who needs a reply, who has a consult coming up, who is waiting on pricing, and which active clients need due-date or referral follow-up.

Open this view

2. Client Board

Client Board

Turns multiple offer paths into one visible flow: new inquiry, consult/reply, offer chosen, and active or aftercare follow-up.

Open this view

3. Individual Client Record

Individual Client Record

Shows what one client page contains: service type, EDD, consult history, notes, checklist items, timeline, and next follow-up.

Open this view

4. Consult + Offer View

Consult + Offer View

A focused view for consult scheduling, offer path, pricing questions, and what has to happen before a lead becomes booked.

Open this view

5. Due Date + Active Support View

Due Date + Active Support View

Organizes active clients and next support steps around due dates instead of a generic all-purpose CRM pipeline.

Open this view

6. Aftercare + Referral View

Aftercare + Referral View

Keeps postpartum check-ins, resource handoffs, and referral asks on the same operating board so warm relationships do not get lost.

Open this view
